docs.title: Core Library Overview
docs.text: Core is an opinionated framework for building robust, production-grade Go desktop applications using the Wails framework. It provides a modular, service-based architecture that simplifies development and ensures maintainability.

docs.title: Key Features
docs.text:
• Modular Architecture: Core is divided into a set of independent services, each responsible for a specific domain (e.g., config, crypt, display).
  • Unified Runtime: A central Runtime object initializes and manages the lifecycle of all services, providing a simple and consistent entry point for your application.
  • Dependency Injection: Services are designed to be testable and decoupled, with dependencies injected at runtime.
  • Standardized Error Handling: A custom error package (pkg/e) provides a consistent way to wrap and handle errors throughout the application.
  • Automated Documentation: This documentation site is automatically generated from the Go source code, ensuring it stays in sync with the public API.

docs.title: Getting Started
docs.text:
To start using the Core library, initialize the runtime in your main.go file:

package main

import (
    "embed"
    "log"

    "github.com/Snider/Core"
    "github.com/wailsapp/wails/v3/pkg/application"
)

//go:embed all:public
var assets embed.FS

func main() {
    app := application.New(application.Options{
        Assets: application.AssetOptions{
            Handler: application.AssetFileServerFS(assets),
        },
    })

    rt, err := core.NewRuntime(app)
    if err != nil {
        log.Fatal(err)
    }

    app.RegisterService(application.NewService(rt))

    err = app.Run()
    if err != nil {
        log.Fatal(err)
    }
}

For more detailed information on each service, see the Services section in the navigation.

docs.title: Service: config
docs.text: The config service provides a unified interface for managing application configuration. It handles retrieving and setting configuration values, persistent storage, and feature flags.

docs.title: type Config
docs.text:
Config defines the contract for the configuration service.

type Config interface {
    // Get retrieves a configuration value by key and stores it in the 'out' variable.
    Get(key string, out any) error

    // Set stores a configuration value by key.
    Set(key string, v any) error
}

docs.title: Standard Implementation
docs.text:
While Config is an interface, the standard implementation typically provides the following functionality:

  • Persistent Storage: Saves configuration to disk (e.g., config.json).
  • Feature Flags: Checking if specific application features are enabled.
  • Defaults: Providing default values for configuration settings.

docs.title: Common Methods
docs.text:
Although not part of the minimal Config interface, implementations often provide:

  • Save() error: Explicitly saves the current configuration to disk.
  • IsFeatureEnabled(feature string) bool: Checks if a feature flag is active.

docs.title: Service: core
docs.text: The core service is the foundation of the application. It serves as a central hub for managing service lifecycles, dependency injection, asset handling, and inter-process communication (IPC).

docs.title: type Core
docs.text:
Core is the central application object. It holds references to the Wails application instance, configuration, and registered services.

type Core struct {
    // App is the Wails application instance
    App *application.App
    // Features manages feature flags
    Features *Features
    // ... internal fields
}

docs.title: type Option
docs.text:
Option is a function type used to configure the Core during initialization.

type Option func(*Core) error

docs.title: type Startable
docs.text:
Startable is an interface for services that require initialization logic when the application starts.

type Startable interface {
    OnStartup(ctx context.Context) error
}

docs.title: type Stoppable
docs.text:
Stoppable is an interface for services that require cleanup logic when the application shuts down.

type Stoppable interface {
    OnShutdown(ctx context.Context) error
}

docs.title: type Message
docs.text:
Message is a marker interface for structs that can be sent via the Core's IPC system.

type Message interface{}

docs.title: type ActionServiceStartup
docs.text:
ActionServiceStartup is a message dispatched when the application services are starting up.

type ActionServiceStartup struct{}

docs.title: type ActionServiceShutdown
docs.text:
ActionServiceShutdown is a message dispatched when the application services are shutting down.

type ActionServiceShutdown struct{}

docs.title: func New(opts ...Option) (*Core, error)
docs.text: New initializes a new Core instance with the provided options. It sets up the internal service registry and feature flags.

docs.title: func WithService(factory func(*Core) (any, error)) Option
docs.text: WithService registers a service using a factory function. It automatically discovers the service name from the package path and registers any IPC handlers if the service implements HandleIPCEvents.

docs.title: func WithName(name string, factory func(*Core) (any, error)) Option
docs.text: WithName registers a service with a specific name using a factory function. Unlike WithService, it does not automatically discover IPC handlers.

docs.title: func WithWails(app *application.App) Option
docs.text: WithWails injects the Wails application instance into the Core. This is required for services that need to interact with the Wails runtime.

docs.title: func WithAssets(fs embed.FS) Option
docs.text: WithAssets registers the application's embedded assets (filesystem).

docs.title: func WithServiceLock() Option
docs.text: WithServiceLock locks the service registry after initialization, preventing further service registration.

docs.title: func ServiceFor[T any](c *Core, name string) (T, error)
docs.text: ServiceFor retrieves a registered service by its name and asserts that it is of type T. Returns an error if the service is not found or the type assertion fails.

docs.title: func MustServiceFor[T any](c *Core, name string) T
docs.text: MustServiceFor is a helper that wraps ServiceFor and panics if the service is not found or the type is incorrect.

docs.title: func App() *application.App
docs.text: App returns the global Wails application instance. It panics if the Core has not been initialized.

docs.title: func (c *Core) Config() Config
docs.text: Config returns the registered Config service. This is a convenience method for accessing application configuration.

docs.title: func (c *Core) Assets() embed.FS
docs.text: Assets returns the embedded filesystem containing the application's assets.

docs.title: func (c *Core) ServiceStartup(ctx context.Context, options application.ServiceOptions) error
docs.text: ServiceStartup is the entry point for the Core's startup lifecycle. It initializes all registered Startable services and dispatches the ActionServiceStartup message.

docs.title: func (c *Core) ServiceShutdown(ctx context.Context) error
docs.text: ServiceShutdown is the entry point for the Core's shutdown lifecycle. It dispatches the ActionServiceShutdown message and calls OnShutdown on all Stoppable services in reverse registration order.

docs.title: func (c *Core) RegisterAction(handler func(*Core, Message) error)
docs.text: RegisterAction registers a new IPC message handler.

docs.title: func (c *Core) RegisterActions(handlers ...func(*Core, Message) error)
docs.text: RegisterActions registers multiple IPC message handlers at once.

docs.title: func (c *Core) ACTION(msg Message) error
docs.text: ACTION dispatches a message to all registered IPC handlers. It aggregates errors from all handlers.

docs.title: func (c *Core) RegisterService(name string, api any) error
docs.text: RegisterService adds a new service to the Core. It detects if the service implements Startable or Stoppable and registers it for lifecycle events.

docs.title: func (c *Core) Service(name string) any
docs.text: Service retrieves a registered service instance by name as an any type. Returns nil if not found.

docs.title: Service: crypt
docs.text: The crypt service provides cryptographic utilities for the application, including hashing, checksums, and PGP encryption/decryption.

docs.title: type HashType
docs.text:
HashType defines the supported hashing algorithms.

type HashType string

docs.title: func EncryptPGP(writer io.Writer, recipientPath, data string, signerPath, signerPassphrase *string) (string, error)
docs.text:
EncryptPGP encrypts data for a specific recipient. - writer: Optional output writer. - recipientPath: Path to the recipient's public key. - data: The data to encrypt. - signerPath: Optional path to a private key to sign the message. - signerPassphrase: Optional passphrase for the signing key.

Returns the encrypted data as a string.

docs.title: func DecryptPGP(recipientPath, message, passphrase string, signerPath *string) (string, error)
docs.text:
DecryptPGP decrypts a PGP message. - recipientPath: Path to the private key for decryption. - message: The encrypted message (armor encoded). - passphrase: Passphrase for the private key. - signerPath: Optional path to the sender's public key to verify the signature.

Returns the decrypted string.

docs.title: func Hash(lib HashType, payload string) string
docs.text: Hash computes a hash of the payload using the specified algorithm (e.g., MD5, SHA256).

docs.title: func Fletcher16(payload string) uint16
docs.text: Fletcher16 computes the Fletcher-16 checksum of the payload.

docs.title: func Fletcher32(payload string) uint32
docs.text: Fletcher32 computes the Fletcher-32 checksum of the payload.

docs.title: func Fletcher64(payload string) uint64
docs.text: Fletcher64 computes the Fletcher-64 checksum of the payload.

docs.title: func Luhn(payload string) bool
docs.text: Luhn validates a number string using the Luhn algorithm (commonly used for credit card numbers).

docs.title: Service: display
docs.text: The display service manages the application's GUI windows, dialogs, and system tray interactions.

docs.title: type ActionOpenWindow
docs.text:
ActionOpenWindow is an IPC message used to request the creation of a new window.

type ActionOpenWindow struct {
    application.WebviewWindowOptions
}

docs.title: type WindowOption
docs.text:
WindowOption is a functional option type for configuring a window during creation.

type WindowOption func(*application.WebviewWindowOptions) error

docs.title: func OpenWindow(opts ...WindowOption) error
docs.text: OpenWindow creates and shows a new window with the specified options.

docs.title: func NewWithURL(url string) (*application.WebviewWindow, error)
docs.text: NewWithURL creates a new window pointing to the specified URL using default settings.

docs.title: func NewWithOptions(opts ...WindowOption) (*application.WebviewWindow, error)
docs.text: NewWithOptions creates a new window by applying a series of WindowOption functions.

docs.title: func SelectDirectory() (string, error)
docs.text: SelectDirectory opens a native directory selection dialog and returns the selected path.

docs.title: func ShowEnvironmentDialog()
docs.text: ShowEnvironmentDialog displays a dialog containing detailed information about the application's runtime environment (OS, version, etc.).

docs.title: func ServiceStartup(ctx context.Context, options application.ServiceOptions) error
docs.text: ServiceStartup initializes the display service, setting up the main window, menu, and system tray.

docs.title: func HandleIPCEvents(c *core.Core, msg core.Message) error
docs.text: HandleIPCEvents processes display-related IPC messages, such as ActionOpenWindow.

docs.title: Service: e
docs.text:
Package e provides a standardized error handling mechanism for the Core library. It allows for wrapping errors with contextual information, making it easier to trace the origin of an error and provide meaningful feedback.

The design of this package is influenced by the need for a simple, yet powerful way to handle errors that can occur in different layers of the application, from low-level file operations to high-level service interactions.

The key features of this package are: - Error wrapping: The Op and an optional Msg field provide context about where and why an error occurred. - Stack traces: By wrapping errors, we can build a logical stack trace that is more informative than a raw stack trace. - Consistent error handling: Encourages a uniform approach to error handling across the entire codebase.

docs.title: type Error
docs.text:
Error represents a standardized error with operational context.

type Error struct {
    // Op is the operation being performed, e.g., "config.Load".
    Op  string
    // Msg is a human-readable message explaining the error.
    Msg string
    // Err is the underlying error that was wrapped.
    Err error
}

docs.title: Methods
docs.text:
• Error() string: Error returns the string representation of the error.
  • Unwrap() error: Unwrap provides compatibility for Go's errors.Is and errors.As functions.

docs.title: Functions
docs.text:
• E(op, msg string, err error) error: E is a helper function to create a new Error.

This is the primary way to create errors that will be consumed by the system. For example:

return e.E("config.Load", "failed to load config file", err)

The op parameter should be in the format of package.function or service.method. The msg parameter should be a human-readable message that can be displayed to the user. The err parameter is the underlying error that is being wrapped.

docs.title: Service: help
docs.text: The help service manages the in-app documentation and help system, allowing users to view guides and context-sensitive help.

docs.title: func Show() error
docs.text: Show opens the main help window or interface.

docs.title: func ShowAt(anchor string) error
docs.text: ShowAt opens the help window and navigates directly to the section specified by the anchor.

docs.title: func HandleIPCEvents(c *core.Core, msg core.Message) error
docs.text: HandleIPCEvents processes help-related IPC messages, allowing other services to trigger help displays.

docs.title: Service: i18n
docs.text: The i18n service handles internationalization and localization, allowing the application to support multiple languages.

docs.title: func SetLanguage(lang string) error
docs.text: SetLanguage sets the active application language. It loads the appropriate message bundle for the specified language tag (e.g., "en-US", "fr").

docs.title: func Translate(messageID string) string
docs.text: Translate retrieves the localized string for the given messageID in the current active language. If no translation is found, it may return the ID or a fallback.

docs.title: func HandleIPCEvents(c *core.Core, msg core.Message) error
docs.text: HandleIPCEvents handles IPC messages related to language changes or translation requests.

docs.title: func ServiceStartup(ctx context.Context, options application.ServiceOptions) error
docs.text: ServiceStartup initializes the i18n service, loading available languages and setting the default locale.

docs.title: Service: io
docs.text: The io service provides a standardized interface for interacting with different storage backends, such as the local disk, S3, or SFTP.

docs.title: type Medium
docs.text:
Medium defines the standard interface for a storage backend.

type Medium interface {
    // Read retrieves the content of a file as a string.
    Read(path string) (string, error)

    // Write saves the given content to a file, overwriting it if it exists.
    Write(path, content string) error

    // EnsureDir makes sure a directory exists, creating it if necessary.
    EnsureDir(path string) error

    // IsFile checks if a path exists and is a regular file.
    IsFile(path string) bool

    // FileGet is a convenience function that reads a file from the medium.
    FileGet(path string) (string, error)

    // FileSet is a convenience function that writes a file to the medium.
    FileSet(path, content string) error
}

docs.title: type MockMedium
docs.text:
MockMedium implements the Medium interface for testing purposes.

type MockMedium struct {
    Files map[string]string
    Dirs  map[string]bool
}

docs.title: Methods
docs.text:
• EnsureDir(path string) error: Mocks creating a directory.
  • FileGet(path string) (string, error): Mocks reading a file.
  • FileSet(path, content string) error: Mocks writing a file.
  • IsFile(path string) bool: Mocks checking if a path is a file.
  • Read(path string) (string, error): Mocks reading a file.
  • Write(path, content string) error: Mocks writing a file.

docs.title: Functions
docs.text:
• Copy(sourceMedium Medium, sourcePath string, destMedium Medium, destPath string) error: Copies a file from a source medium to a destination medium.
  • EnsureDir(m Medium, path string) error: Ensures a directory exists on the given medium.
  • IsFile(m Medium, path string) bool: Checks if a path is a file on the given medium.
  • Read(m Medium, path string) (string, error): Retrieves the content of a file from the given medium.
  • Write(m Medium, path, content string) error: Saves content to a file on the given medium.

Would you like to see some examples of how to use this service?

docs.title: Service: runtime
docs.text: The runtime service provides the main entry point for the application and a helper structure for services to interact with the Core.

docs.title: type Runtime
docs.text:
Runtime is the top-level container that holds the Core instance and the Wails application. It serves as the bridge between Wails and the Core framework.

type Runtime struct {
    // Core is the central service manager
    Core *Core
    // app is the Wails application instance
    app  *application.App
}

docs.title: type ServiceRuntime[T any]
docs.text:
ServiceRuntime is a generic helper struct designed to be embedded in service implementations. It provides easy access to the Core and service-specific options.

type ServiceRuntime[T any] struct {
    core *Core
    opts T
}

docs.title: type ServiceFactory
docs.text:
ServiceFactory is a function type that creates a service instance.

type ServiceFactory func() (any, error)

docs.title: func NewRuntime(app *application.App) (*Runtime, error)
docs.text: NewRuntime creates and wires together all application services using default settings. It is the standard way to initialize the runtime.

docs.title: func NewWithFactories(app *application.App, factories map[string]ServiceFactory) (*Runtime, error)
docs.text: NewWithFactories creates a new Runtime instance using a provided map of service factories. This allows for flexible, dynamic service registration.

docs.title: func NewServiceRuntime[T any](c *Core, opts T) *ServiceRuntime[T]
docs.text: NewServiceRuntime creates a new ServiceRuntime instance. This is typically used in a service's factory or constructor.

docs.title: func (r *Runtime) ServiceName() string
docs.text: ServiceName returns the name of the service ("Core"). This is used by Wails for service identification.

docs.title: func (r *Runtime) ServiceStartup(ctx context.Context, options application.ServiceOptions)
docs.text: ServiceStartup delegates the startup lifecycle event to the underlying Core, which in turn initializes all registered services.

docs.title: func (r *Runtime) ServiceShutdown(ctx context.Context)
docs.text: ServiceShutdown delegates the shutdown lifecycle event to the underlying Core.

docs.title: func (r *ServiceRuntime[T]) Core() *Core
docs.text: Core returns the central Core instance, giving the service access to other services and features.

docs.title: func (r *ServiceRuntime[T]) Config() Config
docs.text: Config returns the registered Config service from the Core. It is a convenience method for accessing configuration.

docs.title: Service: workspace
docs.text: The workspace service manages user workspaces, which are isolated environments for user data and configuration.

docs.title: type Workspace
docs.text:
Workspace represents a single user workspace.

type Workspace struct {
    Name string
    Path string
}

docs.title: func CreateWorkspace(identifier, password string) (string, error)
docs.text:
CreateWorkspace creates a new, secure workspace. - identifier: A unique name or ID for the workspace. - password: A password used to secure the workspace (if encryption is supported).

Returns the workspace ID or path.

docs.title: func SwitchWorkspace(name string) error
docs.text: SwitchWorkspace changes the currently active workspace to the one specified by name.

docs.title: func WorkspaceFileGet(filename string) (string, error)
docs.text: WorkspaceFileGet retrieves the content of a file located within the active workspace.

docs.title: func WorkspaceFileSet(filename, content string) error
docs.text: WorkspaceFileSet writes content to a file within the active workspace.

docs.title: func HandleIPCEvents(c *core.Core, msg core.Message) error
docs.text: HandleIPCEvents processes workspace-related IPC messages.

docs.title: func ServiceStartup(ctx context.Context, options application.ServiceOptions) error
docs.text: ServiceStartup initializes the workspace service and loads the list of available workspaces.
